summary: Iteration 26 — End-to-end testing for new Health Explorer dashboard + Reports Builder read-only for VIEWER. Backend: 10/10 pytest pass (test_health_explorer.py) covering L1/L2/L4 drill, asset_type/station modes, SA global scope, SUP scoping=50 assets (DHN+Electrical), VIEW001 global=SA scope, filters endpoint (SUP sees 1 station, 1 dept, 5 asset_types), filter narrowing. Frontend: SA dashboard shows 2 tabs (tab-health-explorer default + tab-classic-dashboard). HealthExplorer renders with summary card (30.1% healthy · 22 of 73), bucket chips, mode toggle (he-mode-type/station), 3 filter chips, refresh button. Mode toggle to 'station' persists to localStorage('health-explorer-mode') and survives reload (station button retains font-semibold). SA Reports Builder: Save/Run/CSV/Excel/PDF/Dossier-Save all VISIBLE. VIEW001 Reports Builder: Builder tab VISIBLE, no 'Superadmin only' message, builder-save-btn HIDDEN ✓, dossier-save-btn HIDDEN ✓, but Run/CSV/Excel/PDF/Dossier-Export-PDF VISIBLE ✓. VIEWER badge shows 'Audit Viewer · Viewer (Read-only)'. SUP (SSE001) Health Explorer summary correctly shows '20 of 50' assets — matches expected DHN+Electrical scope.

issues: At 30.1% healthy global, bars are all rendered red (#dc2626) — correct per threshold but visually dominating. Consider a subtle bar-track background so 0% bars still show a cylinder outline (currently 0% rows show only the cap indicator). Non-blocking.

action_items: No blocking issues. Main agent may self-verify L1→L2→L3→L4 drill in browser by clicking a CylinderBar row; backend drill verified by pytest is identical to the path the onSelect handler triggers.

Optional: add data-testid='he-bar-row-{id}' on each CylinderBar row to make drill clicks scriptable in future Playwright tests (current SVG/g elements have no cursor-pointer class exposed at the row level — manual click via screen coordinates works, automated click via [data-testid] would be cleaner).
critical_code_review_comments: health_explorer.py is clean (~275 lines), reuses _load_universe / _filter_assets_for_user / _classify from reports.py — no duplication, good separation. Color thresholds are inline-duplicated with frontend; consider exporting from a shared constants module if more places need them.

HealthExplorer.js correctly resets drill on mode change (useEffect line 98) and on any filter change (goHome() in MultiSelectChip onChange). LocalStorage persistence is wrapped in try/catch — defensive, good.

ReportsBuilderPage.js: canLoad/canWrite gating is clean (lines 565-568). Save / delete / dossier-save all gated via {canWrite && ...} blocks. Run + Export buttons remain unconditional — correct read-only behaviour for viewer.
ReportsPage.js line 467: showBuilder = isSA || isViewer — minimal and clear. Tab and TabsContent both gated on same flag — no leaky state.

success_rate.frontend: 95% — all testids present, all role-based hide/show flows verified; only L1→L2 drill click could not be automated due to missing row-level data-testid (drill verified by backend pytest instead)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Health Explorer is the new default dashboard tab at /. Backend endpoint: GET /api/dashboard/health-explorer/{user_id} with optional ?mode=station|asset_type, ?asset_type_id, ?station_id, ?location_id, ?station_ids, ?dept_ids, ?asset_type_ids. Filter list: GET /api/dashboard/health-explorer/{user_id}/filters. SA sees 73 total assets, SUP (SSE001) sees 50 (DHN+Electrical), VIEW001 sees 73 (global, read-only). Frontend testids: tab-health-explorer, tab-classic-dashboard, health-explorer, he-summary-card, he-mode-type, he-mode-station, he-filter-stations, he-filter-depts, he-filter-types, he-refresh, he-breadcrumb. localStorage key: 'health-explorer-mode'. CylinderBar SVG rows lack a per-row data-testid — request main agent to add if more granular Playwright testing is needed. Reports Builder: builder-save-btn, dossier-save-btn are gated by canWrite (SA only). builder-run-btn, builder-export-csv/excel/pdf, dossier-export-pdf/excel are always visible.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Both features (Health Explorer default dashboard + Reports Builder read-only for viewer) work as specified.
